focus_points.label: Clarity on expectations and what good means.
focus_points.reason: Whether he feels clear on what “good” looks like for a junior product designer right now, especially with shifting requirements—worth clarifying whether the bar and success criteria feel stable or keep moving.

focus_points.label: Feeling safe asking for help and support.
focus_points.reason: How he’s landing with the team’s day-to-day support—whether he feels he can ask questions early without slowing people down, and whether the team setup is making that easier or harder.

focus_points.label: Frustration level and confidence trend over time.
focus_points.reason: Notes suggest a dip around three months in and possible frustration with shifting requirements; one possibility is stress from ambiguity, another is confidence lag—worth clarifying what he’s feeling hardest right now and how it’s changing week to week.

focus_points.label: What’s hardest right now with shifting requirements.
focus_points.reason: Whether the work he’s saying yes to matches the team’s current bets, or whether shifting requirements are pulling him into churn—worth mapping what he’s optimizing for and what keeps getting reprioritized.
